implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for VACUUM operational-excellence posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for VACUUM.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for VACUUM.
data_model: vacuum_operational-excellence_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for VACUUM operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for VACUUM; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for VACUUM.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for VACUUM recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for VACUUM.
acceptance_criteria: Given VACUUM data exists, when the operational-excellence automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: VACUUM connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate table bloat performance issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for table bloat.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for table bloat.
data_model: table-bloat_performance_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for table bloat performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for table bloat; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for table bloat.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for table bloat recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for table bloat.
acceptance_criteria: Given table bloat data exists, when the performance agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: table bloat connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for unused indexes performance posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for unused indexes.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for unused indexes.
data_model: unused-indexes_performance_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for unused indexes performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for unused indexes; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for unused indexes.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for unused indexes recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for unused indexes.
acceptance_criteria: Given unused indexes data exists, when the performance automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: unused indexes connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
